pages.text:
Post-Meeting
Summaries
AI Transparency Technical Note
March 2024
© 2023 Cisco and/or its affiliates. All rights reserve.

Page 3 of 7
At Cisco, we appreciate that Artificial Intelligence (AI) can be leveraged to power an inclusive
future for all. We also recognize that by applying this technology, we have a responsibility to
mitigate potential harm. That is why Cisco adheres to our Responsible AI Framework (the
“Framework”), which is based on six principles of Transparency, Fairness, Accountability, Privacy,
Security and Reliability. Cisco translates these principles into product development requirements,
which ultimately form part of the product development lifecycle alongside our Security by Design,
Privacy by Design, and Human Rights by Design processes.
Accordingly, Webex by Cisco (Webex) features that leverage AI are built with transparency,
fairness, accountability, privacy, security, and reliability at their core. Each
feature powered by AI undergoes an AI Impact (AII) Assessment — a best-in-class review of how
the technical underpinnings of the functionality measure against the Framework principles.
Post-Meeting Summaries was built with the Framework Principles at the center of how we deliver
the AI-powered technology. This Technical Note describes more information about the feature,
and how we responsibly leverage AI to deliver the functionality. Post-Meeting Summaries is an
optional feature; organization administrators can disable or allow individual users to choose
whether, and to what extent, they use the feature.
© 2023 Cisco and/or its affiliates. All rights reserve.

pages.text:
Page 4 of 7
Feature Overview
Post-Meeting Summaries generates a meeting summary, comprised of meeting notes, action
items, and chapters after a meeting has concluded. The Meeting Notes section includes the
meeting agenda, decisions that were made during the meeting, notable events that occurred
during the meeting, and valuable information that was shared. The Action Items section lists
relevant action items and tasks, the person(s) responsible for tasks, and the general category
of the task. Post-Meeting Summaries also creates automatic meeting chapters so users can
easily switch between topics that were discussed in the meeting.
Figure 1: Post-Meeting Summaries
Model Overview
Introduction
Post-Meeting Summaries is currently powered by Cisco AI technologies and third-party large
language models (LLM) provided by the Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service. The underlying LLM
functionality utilizes Microsoft’s shared infrastructure, with certain configuration and
enablement by Cisco. For more information about the Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service see
Transparency Note for Azure OpenAI Service.
Post-Meeting Summaries is deployed on the Webex platform as part of the Webex App. It is
also accessible through the Webex site where the meeting was hosted.
© 2023 Cisco and/or its affiliates. All rights reserve.

```markdown
## Visual Elements Summary
- Process flow diagram illustrating AI service interactions
- Security and cloud environment annotations
- Icons for HTTPS, automated content moderation, and cloud environments
- Numbered steps for user interaction flow
- Textual callouts for security and operational notes

---

## Diagrams and Visuals

### Diagram: Post-Meeting Summaries Process Flow
**Components:**
1. **Webex App** (User initiation point)
2. **Messaging AI Service** and **Meetings AI Service** (Cisco controlled cloud environment)
3. **Language AI Service** (Cisco controlled cloud environment)
4. **Azure OpenAI Services** (Azure controlled multi-tenant cloud configured by Cisco)

**Connections:**
- **HTTPS connections** secured between components.
- **Automated content moderation** icons present in Azure OpenAI Services.
- **Stateless operations** annotation indicating no customer data is persisted or logged.

**Numbered Steps:**
1. User initiates an AI-powered feature from the application.
2. Messaging or Meetings AI Service decrypts content, collects relevant information, and forwards it for processing by the AI.
3. Language AI Service manages the secure connection to AI models to perform the AI function.
4. Generated response is sent back to the application to fulfill the user's request.

**Security and Operational Notes:**
- **HTTPS** connections secured.
- **Automated content moderation** in Azure OpenAI Services.
- **Stateless operations** with no customer data persisted or logged in these systems.
- **Cisco controlled cloud environment** for Messaging AI Service, Meetings AI Service, and Language AI Service.
- **Azure controlled multi-tenant cloud** configured by Cisco for Azure OpenAI Services.

---

## Data and Numeric Information
- No explicit numeric data or metrics are presented in the visuals.

## Data Visualization
- **Flowchart** for illustrating the process flow of AI services and interactions.

## Data Representation
- **Icons** for security and operational features.
- **Textual annotations** for detailed explanations of each step and security measures.

## Data Sources
- **Cisco and Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service** for AI functionalities.
- **Webex platform** for deployment and accessibility.

---
```

pages.text:
Page 5 of 7
Model Architecture
Post-Meeting Summaries leverages a self-hosted in-house model in conjunction with GPT-
3.5-Turbo via the Microsoft Azure OpenAI Service API to generate the meeting summaries.
Usage Guidelines
To enable Post-Meeting Summaries, the meeting host must enable meeting recording. The
meeting summary generated is stored with the meeting recording and transcript, which allows
users to easily verify the contents of the summary, if required.
Currently, Post-Meeting Summaries supports only English language meetings. Post-Meeting
Summaries cannot be used outside of Webex applications, Cisco devices, or Webex
developers SDKs.
Model Inputs and Outputs
A typical input to the models underlying Post-Meeting Summaries is a transcript of a meeting.
Typical output is a summary of the meeting that includes Meeting Notes, Action Items, and
Chapters. For a 1-hour meeting, the output summary is approximately five hundred words.
Action items, including assigned participants, and Chapters will be presented separately from
the Meeting Notes section.
Data Sources for Training and Evaluation
Cisco does not use customer content to fine tune or train the models used to deliver Post-
Meeting Summaries. Microsoft represents that it does not use customer data to improve
Azure OpenAI models. Microsoft also represents that it does not store or retain Cisco
customer data in the Azure infrastructure. For more information about Microsoft’s data,
privacy, and security practices associated with the Azure OpenAI Service, see Data, privacy,
and security for Azure OpenAI Service.
The Microsoft Azure OpenAI models are trained on a large dataset compiled from various
sources. This dataset consists of a wide range of text, including books, articles, websites, and
other publicly available written material. The training data encompasses a diverse array of
topics and writing styles to help the model develop a broad understanding of language and its
contexts. For further information, see OpenAI GPT Models.
© 2023 Cisco and/or its affiliates. All rights reserve.

data: t it does not use c

pages.text:
Page 6 of 7
Model Evaluation and Performance
Cisco frequently assesses Post-Meeting Summaries to maintain and improve both
performance and accuracy of generated output. Humans are involved with the review, testing,
and quality assurance associated with the deployment of the underlying model.
Safety and Ethical Considerations
The Post-Meeting Summaries model may output toxic, harmful or unsafe content if such
content is present in the meeting(s) being summarized. However, the Azure OpenAI Service
includes content filtering, which is enabled by Cisco and can help mitigate that issue. Microsoft
also attempts to improve mathematical reasoning with process supervision.
In addition to leveraging these technical mitigations, Webex has also taken additional steps to
mitigate safety risks and ensure ethical use. For example, Webex third-party vendors,
including Microsoft, undergo rigorous vendor reviews, which include security and safety
assessments. The summary generated by Post-Meeting Summaries is visible only to the user
of the feature unless that user voluntarily.
Please contact Cisco if you have concerns or feedback about your experience with Post-
Meeting Summaries.
Fairness
For more information on how the model underlying Post-Meeting Summaries is trained for
fairness, please reference Transparency Note for Azure OpenAI Service
Privacy and Security
Webex addresses processing of and security around personal data, including retention periods
for that data of personal data, in its Privacy Data Sheets, found on the Cisco Trust Portal.
Cisco does not retain the input data after the inference process but does store the outcome of
the inference until the user makes a new summary request.
© 2023 Cisco and/or its affiliates. All rights reserve.

Page 7 of 7
Microsoft represents that it does not access, monitor, or store Cisco customer data.
Regarding Cisco access to customer personal data, access rights are limited, and encryption is
standard, as indicated in the relevant Privacy Data Sheet.
Updates and Maintenance
Ongoing Webex updates and product release documents include product updates and any
improvements in user experience. Webex may also use customer email notifications and
Webex Help Center documentation to inform customers of product changes.
